latest.title: Distribution Partnership
latest.content:
30 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4779Y
T42 IOT Tracking Solutions PLC
30 March 2026
REACH
t42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc("t42" or the "Company")
New Distribution Partnership in the Nordic Region
Expansion of Global Reach
t42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc ("t42" or the "Company"), a leading provider of real-time tracking, monitoring and security solutions for the global container and freight market, announces a new distribution partnership with M2M Nordic ApS ("M2M Nordic") in Denmark, with the potential to expand into a broader collaboration across the Nordic region.
This partnership combines t42's proven IoT tracking and monitoring technologies, built on over 30 years of experience and with more than one million units deployed worldwide, with M2M Nordic's strong regional presence and expertise in connectivity and IoT integration.
Together, the companies aim to deliver scalable, high-performance solutions for asset tracking, smart security and real-time data visibility across a range of industries utilising the Company's Lokies, Tetis and Kylos MC products, and its software solutions. The partnership represents a further step in t42's strategy to expand its global footprint and strengthen its position within the rapidly growing IoT ecosystem.
M2M Nordic is a Denmark-based trading and solutions company with more than 20 years of experience in infrastructure, applications and telecommunications, with a particular focus on mobile network technologies and evolving market trends. The company specialises in reliable and cost-efficient communication solutions within the M2M and IoT sectors, offering high-quality products from leading manufacturers. M2M Nordic's ambition is to support increased digitalisation across industries by delivering tailored, future-ready solutions for both existing and emerging applications.
Avi Hartmann, CEO of t42, commented
:
"We are delighted with this agreement as we look to further expand our global footprint in high growth regions. We believe that there is a compelling backdrop for our solutions and look forward further growing our routes to market via low-risk, low-cost partnerships globally. We have established strong foundations for growth, with a world-class product suite incorporating both hardware and software solutions. We are delighted with the increased levels of traction and look forward to providing further updates in due course."
Contacts:

t42@walbrookpr.com

Notes to Editors
t42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc
(AIM: TRAC), formerly Starcom Systems plc, provides real-time tracking, analysis, monitoring, and security IoT solutions for the global container and freight market and covers 55 countries, over 100 distributors, and 50 logistics and support partners.
t42's multi-sensor IoT tracking devices use a wide range of detection capabilities with cloud-based analytics and alerts, with real-time data transmission, analysis, and actionable insights. Its devices are used by ports, cargo owners, shipping companies, freight forwarders, insurance companies, customs authorities, homeland security, and police for end-to-end global container tracking and digital transformation of shipments.
For more information on the Company, please visit:
www.t42.co.uk/
.

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
9 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7368V
T42 IOT Tracking Solutions PLC
09 March 2026
t42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc("t42" or the "Company")
Full-Year Trading Update and Notice of Final Results
Strong margin and revenue growth
Continued progression towards sustained profitability
T42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc (AIM: TRAC) ("t42" or the "Company"), a leading provider of global shipping container tracking solutions, announces a trading update for the year ended 31 December 2025 (the "Period").
Financial Highlights (unaudited)
Revenue for the Period increased significantly to over $6.1m (2024: $4.16m), including a c.10% increase in SaaS revenues, highlighting strong trading across all parts of the business. Gross margins grew to c.45% (2024: 38%), reflecting the continued reduction of production costs and increased sales from higher margin products, notably Lokies. The Company is pleased to report an operating profit for the period of c.$0.4m, a substantial improvement on the prior year (2024: operating loss of $0.9m), with the Company achieving positive EBITDA of c.$1.05m (2024: EBITDA loss of $0.2m), reflecting the Board's focus on progressing towards sustained profitability.
Operational Highlights
The Company's products and technology continue to attract interest worldwide, with particular emphasis on the range of Lokies secure padlocks. Furthermore, the Company's innovative technology and solutions continue to underpin growing demand from a range of geographies and end customers, providing increasing levels of visibility. Importantly the Company is well placed to benefit from growing levels of pipeline conversion, SaaS revenues and long-term orders, underpinning management's confidence in delivering significant shareholder value.
Outlook
The Company is seeing the benefits of establishing long-term relationships with customers globally. Given the global nature of the customer base and location of manufacturing facilities, the Company foresees minimal impact from the current turmoil in the Middle East and remains confident that its pipeline and ability to service customers remain unaffected.
The strong momentum generated during the Period is expected to be reflected further during the current year. Increased levels of commercialisation are expected as the Company further develops both existing and new client relationships.
Notice of Results
Audited accounts for the Period are expected to be published during April 2026, with the notice of Annual General Meeting expected shortly thereafter.

latest.title: AGM Results, Director Dealings, Change of Auditor
latest.content:
5 Jan 2026 11:45
RNS Number : 6742N
T42 IOT Tracking Solutions PLC
05 January 2026
5 January 2026
t42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc
("t42" or the "Company")
Result of AGM
Grant of options, Issue of Director shares, Change of Auditor
T42 IoT Tracking Solutions plc (AIM: TRAC) ("t42" or the "Company"), a leading provider of global shipping container tracking solutions, is pleased to announce that at the Company's Annual General Meeting ("AGM"), held on 31 December 2025, all resolutions were duly passed. The proxy voting results will shortly be available on the Company's website at:
www.t42.co.uk/
.
Grant of options
Following shareholder approval at the AGM, the Company, on 2 January 2026 granted options over ordinary shares in the Company ("Options") to the following Directors and PDMRs:
Name
Quantity
Exercise price
Vesting Period
Avi Hartmann
250,000
2.5p
12 months
Avi Hartmann
250,000
3.0p
2 years, equally
Avi Hartmann
250,000
4.0p
3 years, equally
Uri Hartmann (PDMR)
200,000
2.5p
12 months
Michael Rosenberg
75,000
2.5p
12 months
Martin Blair
75,000
2.5p
12 months
Aviran Sabag (PDMR)
150,000
2.5p
2 years, equally
The Options shall vest, subject to continued employment, as set out in the schedule above and shall lapse 10 years after grant.
Issue of Director Shares
At the AGM, shareholders approved proposals in relation to outstanding unpaid remuneration due to Michael Rosenberg, Chairman, and Martin Blair, non-executive director.
Consequently, the Company is issuing 4,003,925 new ordinary shares to Mr Rosenberg and 3,525,471 new ordinary shares to Mr Blair in lieu of a portion (c. 76%) of their outstanding renumeration, calculated by reference to a price of 2p per ordinary share, being a premium of c.10% to the closing price of t42 ordinary shares on the day prior to the Notice of AGM. The new ordinary shares shall be issued using the Company's existing share authorities. Following issue of the new ordinary shares, Mr Rosenberg's holding comprises 6,561,294 ordinary shares and Mr Blair's holding comprises 4,102,884 ordinary shares, representing 9.0% and 5.6%, respectively, of the Company's enlarged issued ordinary share capital.
The balance of outstanding remuneration due to Mr Rosenburg and Mr Blair shall be repaid in cash in equal monthly instalments over the next 24 months.
Application will be made for the admission of the 7,529,396 new ordinary shares to trading on AIM ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the Placing Shares on AIM will commence at 8.00 a.m. on or around 9 January 2026. The new ordinary shares will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares.
On Admission, the Company's issued share capital will consist of 73,155,753 ordinary shares, each with one voting right. There are no ordinary shares held in treasury. Following Admission, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 73,155,753 and this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
PDMR Loan interest
At the AGM, shareholders approved an increase in the interest payable on the $256,000 loan to the Company by Uri Hartmann, Chief Technical Officer, in 2017, which remains outstanding, from 4% to 8% per annum, effective 1 January 2026.
Change of Auditor
At the AGM, shareholders approved the appointment of KPMG Israel as Auditor of the Company with effect from 31 December 2025.

company_30s.what_they_do: Trac Ltd. builds software and hardware that lets shippers and logistics managers see where every container is in real‑time, plus analytics to predict delays and optimise routes.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global shipping network is worth trillions and every delay costs freight companies millions. A reliable tracking platform cuts those losses and gives carriers a competitive edge. Trac’s IoT sensors and cloud dashboard make it easier for shippers to plan and for regulators to monitor cargo flow.
company_30s.current_state: The business has been reshuffling its board, changing auditors and bringing in a new broker to improve transparency and liquidity. Recent trading updates show modest revenue growth, but the company is still burning cash as it scales its technology. The share trades near the lower end of its valuation range, leaving room for upside if the platform gains traction.

rns_narrative: 1) Governance and financial health – AGM results, director transactions, a new auditor and a fresh broker all point to an effort to tighten controls and improve credibility.
2) Operational momentum – the latest shipping‑container‑tracking update and a convertible‑loan extension give the company breathing room to keep its technology running while it looks for new capital.
3) Performance signals – interim results and a trading‑update release suggest the company is still working on profitability but is stabilising its cash flow.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Muted optimism in a quiet market
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders say the platform is finally gaining traction and that a funding round could give the stock a clean break.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to weak demand for tracking services and the company’s heavy cash burn as reasons to stay away.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to slightly bullish after a few posts about a possible funding announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public search interest. This means a news event could catch everyone off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: Upcoming earnings release – a better‑than‑expected revenue or a clear roadmap could spark a bounce.
Potential closing of the convertible‑loan extension – a fresh capital injection would reduce debt pressure and signal confidence.
Industry news – any policy that boosts container traffic (e.g., new port expansions) would lift demand for Trac’s services.
Volume spike at the current support level – a buying wave would confirm that the market has started to see value.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20‑day moving‑average break above the current price, a 30‑day volume surge, and a positive earnings surprise. Any of these would validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price sits at the bottom of a deep decline. APEX compression suggests the market is ready to release the tension. We’re in the early recovery window – a single catalyst could unlock a 4‑ to 6‑fold move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If Trac is truly 80% below its all‑time high, a rebound to 50% of that peak would be a 5× gain from today. The system’s historical win rate for deep crashes is 40‑50%, so a few large wins offset many small losses, making the upside attractive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Liquidity risk remains – the share is thinly traded and could stay in a low‑volume trough. If the company fails to secure funding or if container traffic stalls, the price could stay near its current level or fall further, leaving only a 10‑20% recovery at best.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 5×. Potential downside: 10‑20% of current price. The asymmetry favours the bullish case if a catalyst appears.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce that breaks the 20‑day average with a stop 10% below that breakout. Size the position so that a 10% drop would consume no more than 2% of your account.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until earnings or a funding announcement. If the price climbs 10% with volume >1.5× the 20‑day average, consider adding more.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately. Otherwise, maintain the bullish stance.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Trac sits at the bottom of a deep crash; a funding or earnings catalyst could trigger a 4‑ to 6‑fold rebound, giving a highly asymmetric upside while the downside is limited to a modest pullback.
